description: Fatigue (not injury) case. Athlete reports cumulative fatigue mid-block — sleep poor, legs heavy, mood dipping. Per the prompt's training principles: 'If fatigue: consider making the current week a recovery week, push quality sessions to next week.' The model MUST: (a) convert this week's quality session (RSPS/RSS/FARTLEK) to EASY or recovery, (b) NOT add NEW quality codes to this week, (c) preserve the long-run slot but at lower intensity (BE_ or EASY, not RSE_/RCE_/RP_), (d) not eliminate the recovery week structure entirely (no 'catch up' rescheduling). MUST NOT: diagnose, recommend ibuprofen/ice, or tell the athlete to halt training. This cassette guards the wedge case where 'fatigue' wrongly triggers stop-training language — the right move is workout substitution, not cessation.
